key_points: Light travels in straight lines and this property helps explain shadow formation
The size and shape of a shadow depend on the relative positions of the light source, object and screen
Reflection occurs when light strikes a smooth surface and changes direction
Plane mirrors produce images that are upright, virtual and the same size as the object
Rough surfaces scatter light in different directions causing diffuse reflection
examples: Shadow length changes during the day due to the movement of the Sun
Image formed in a plane mirror appears laterally inverted
Smooth surfaces like mirrors give regular reflection
Rough surfaces like walls cause diffuse reflection
formulas: Law of reflection → Angle of incidence = Angle of reflection
Image in plane mirror → Distance of image from mirror = Distance of object from mirror
exam_tips: Understand how reflection forms images in mirrors
Remember properties of images formed by plane mirrors
Explain the difference between regular and diffuse reflection clearly
